error.signature: UnicodeEncodeError: 'ascii' codec can't encode characters — Korean folder in sys.path

verdict.summary: Python process fails when project lives in a Korean-named directory (e.g. /home/user/프로젝트) and locale is not UTF-8.

dead_ends.why_fails: Works but does not fix the root cause; other Korean paths will still break
PYTHONIOENCODING affects stdout/stderr encoding, not filesystem path encoding

workarounds.action: Set locale to UTF-8: export LANG=en_US.UTF-8 (or ko_KR.UTF-8) and ensure locale-gen is run
workarounds.how: export LANG=ko_KR.UTF-8; sudo locale-gen ko_KR.UTF-8  # fixes filesystem encoding

workarounds.action: Set PYTHONUTF8=1 environment variable (Python 3.7+ UTF-8 mode)
workarounds.how: export PYTHONUTF8=1  # forces UTF-8 for all file operations regardless of locale
